content.problemStatement:
JJ challenges his friend GG to construct an array AAA containing NNN **distinct** elements such that the following conditions hold:


For all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N, 1≤Ai≤1051 \le A_i \le 10^51≤Ai​≤105
For every subarray of length ≥2\ge 2≥2, the sum of all the elements of the subarray is not divisible by the length of the subarray
Please help perplexed GG to complete JJ's challenge.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA to be constructed.


Output Format
For each test case, output an array AAA containing NNN distinct elements which satisfy the given conditions.


If there are multiple arrays that satisfy the conditions, print any.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case-1: Following are the subarrays of length ≥2\ge 2≥2:

Length=2Length = 2Length=2: sum([7,2])=9sum([7, 2]) = 9sum([7,2])=9, sum([2,5])=7sum([2, 5]) = 7sum([2,5])=7
Length=3Length = 3Length=3: sum([7,2,5])=14sum([7, 2, 5]) = 14sum([7,2,5])=14

We can see that for each of these subarrays, the sum is not divisible by the length.
Test case-2: Following are the subarrays of length ≥2\ge 2≥2:

Length=2Length = 2Length=2: sum([3,18])=21sum([3, 18]) = 21sum([3,18])=21, sum([18,11])=29sum([18, 11]) = 29sum([18,11])=29, sum([11,2])=13sum([11, 2]) = 13sum([11,2])=13
Length=3Length = 3Length=3: sum([3,18,11])=32sum([3, 18, 11]) = 32sum([3,18,11])=32, sum([18,11,2])=31sum([18, 11, 2]) = 31sum([18,11,2])=31
Length=4Length = 4Length=4: sum([3,18,11,2])=34sum([3, 18, 11, 2]) = 34sum([3,18,11,2])=34

We can see that for each of these subarrays, the sum is not divisible by the length.
